comments.body: @giuseppe Do you want to take over my PR and replace it with this one?  I am fine with that.
@rhatdan I am fine with that.  Tests seem happy now so we can pass to the review
@giuseppe  I think I just fixed the travis issues, but I think you need to rebase your PR to fix it.
bot, retest this please

@giuseppe You need to add some tests.  Otherwise LGTM
@mheon @baude @TomSweeneyRedHat @umohnani8 @nalind PTAL
Code LGTM, but would like a head nod from @mheon.  +1 to @rhatdan's request for tests.  The man pages could use a little help, I made a few comments there.

I'm leery of all the special handling for the separate mount namespace for user namespace containers. We have a lot of things already in place to deal with where parts of a container are located (in the database, as `c.state.MountPoint`, `c.state.RunDir`, and as `c.bundlePath()`. We should be using these instead of adding separate handling. `mountContainer()` should ensure the temporary directory we're mounting everything into exists and is appropriately chown'd and set paths in the database appropriately, and then we can use the existing code to determine where to put everything.

the additional bind mounts dance was necessary as an alternative to https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/171, https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/172 and https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/173 :-) as a non-root user would not have any access to the directories containers/storage gives to us

@giuseppe I don't really mind the bind mounts, I just want less special handling for them. We should try and make them work with the existing conventions where possible, as opposed to making conditional changes to where we store things.

If possible, I'd like to use `bundlePath()` to still reference the location where the container's final mount point, the one we're using to launch the final container with `runc`, will live. We can make another function, `containerFilesPath()`, that will reference where files that need to be mounted into the container, e.g. `/dev/shm`, `/etc/resolv.conf`, will be stored. This should allow for a lot of the extra conditional handling of where to put files, e.g. in `makeBindMounts()` and around secrets, to be dropped.
I am not sure the userNS tests can pass on the old kernel used by the CI.  They pass locally
Do you mean on RHEL systems?
no, I think Travis is running on Trusty
I'll install Trusty locally tomorrow and try to debug it
We could conditional-disable the user namespace tests if they run on Travis.
Yes lets do that.  No need to run it on an ancient kernel.
bot, retest this please
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably e6ec1aa) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
I disabled the new tests on Travis for now :arrow_up:

Ok we have green buttons.

@umohnani8 @mheon @TomSweeneyRedHat @baude @nalind PTAL
@mheon I've pushed a fixup that reduces the amount of conditional cases for the userNS case.
@giuseppe Sign the PR Please.
Got a few more comments, but overall looks a lot better.
tests are happy once again!

LGTM
@rh-atomic-bot r+

:pushpin: Commit a46f6ce has been approved by `mheon`

:zap: Test exempted: merge already tested.
This is awesome.  Giuseppe, could you start a google doc for a blog on using podman an userns together.  I think we need to make a big splash on this next week.
